news.title: One lakh Ghaziabad's property owners yet to pay tax, revenue collection dips
news.para: Ghaziabad Municipal Corporation collected Rs 305 crore in property tax for the fiscal year 2025-26. This amount fell short by Rs 35 crore compared to the previous year. A dispute between the mayor and municipal commissioner over a property tax rate hike confused over one lakh residents. The state government is expected to provide a clear direction soon.

news.title: Small cities like Mangalore, Bhopal, Aurangabad join GCC race
news.para: Global capability centres are expanding into India's tier-II cities like Mangalore, Bhopal, and Aurangabad. These emerging tech hubs offer cost advantages and skilled talent. States are actively promoting these locations with supportive policies and infrastructure. This trend is creating new job opportunities and attracting professionals to smaller cities. The growth signifies a structured expansion of tech operations across India.

news.title: Goa health minister puts all Prabhu Realtors' projects on hold
news.para: Goa's Health Minister Vishwajit Rane has ordered a halt on all real estate project approvals for Prabhu Realtors. This action follows water contamination and other lapses at their Chicalim housing complex. Rane stated that no files from business partner Amit Prabhu will be cleared until these issues are rectified, emphasizing that fraudulent claims by builders will not be tolerated.

news.title: Bengaluru development body may regularise up to 15% deviation from building plan
news.para: Bengaluru property owners facing occupancy certificate issues due to minor plan deviations will find relief as the Greater Bengaluru Authority proposes to increase the permissible limit from 5% to 15%. This policy shift aims to regularize constructions with compounding fees, provided safety and structural integrity are met. The move acknowledges practical challenges in dense urban development.

news.title: NHB flags ₹933 crore additional bad loans in PNB Housing Finance's books
news.para: PNB Housing Finance has been directed by the National Housing Bank to disclose an additional ₹933 crore in bad loans for March 2023. This divergence arose from non-compliant restructuring of two project finance loan accounts in FY22-23, where interest rates were reduced. The company states there is no current financial impact.

news.title: Noida authority to consider new mixed land use property scheme in upcoming board meeting
news.para: Noida Authority's upcoming board meeting will focus on its budget and a new mixed land use property scheme. A key discussion point will be the Centre of Excellence at Gautam Buddha University, with a decision pending on whether it will research semiconductors or stem cells. The Authority plans to increase land rates and its budget this year.

news.title: New circle rates for FY27 come into force in Gurugram
news.para: New circle rates for properties in Gurugram have come into effect, with increases ranging from 15% to 30% across residential, commercial, and agricultural sectors. Some rapidly developing areas saw collector rates surge by up to 75%, reflecting prevailing market conditions and infrastructure development. The revision aims to ensure transparency and align rates with actual market values.

news.title: HC stays Haryana 'stilt-plus-four policy' in residential areas
news.para: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has temporarily halted Haryana's "stilt-plus-4 policy," which permits four-storey houses in residential areas. This interim order, effective until a final decision, follows petitions challenging the policy. Concerns were raised about the strain on existing infrastructure, particularly in Gurgaon, due to increased building density.

news.title: Sunrays Heights moves Haryana RERA against buyers for payment defaults, plea rejected
news.para: Haryana Rera has dismissed a developer's attempt to claim extra compensation from homebuyers. The developer had sought unpaid installments and additional damages. Rera ruled that the builder-buyer agreement already outlines remedies for payment defaults. The authority stated that contractual rights cannot be expanded through regulatory forums. Both parties will bear their own costs.

news.title: Tainted officials, twin towers & now land payouts, corruption shadow on Noida authority again
news.para: Noida Authority officials face fresh allegations of demanding a 10% cut from farmer compensation. This brings the development body under judicial review again. Past scandals involving land allotment fraud, maintenance contract scams, and builder-official collusion have repeatedly tarnished its image. The Supreme Court has previously criticized the authority for corruption and misuse of power.

news.title: TVS ILP signs ₹200 crore MoU with Karnataka
news.para: TVS Industrial & Logistics Parks will invest ₹200 crore in Karnataka. This will create new tech-enabled logistics and industrial parks. This development supports India's growing industrial and logistics real estate sector. Leasing activity shows strong year-on-year growth.

news.title: Marathon Nextgen Realty acquires majority stake in three entities for ₹70 crore
news.para: Marathon Nextgen Realty has secured a controlling stake in three real estate firms. This acquisition involves six residential projects with significant development potential. The company gains control over project execution.  Many projects are under construction or set for launch soon.

news.title: Visakhapatnam civic body collects ₹620 crore in property tax for FY26
news.para: Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation (GVMC) achieved a significant 22% increase in property and vacant land tax collection, reaching Rs 620 crore for 2025-26. This surge, driven by expanded tax bases and focused vacant land assessments, surpassed initial targets. Enhanced administrative efforts and a 50% interest waiver also contributed to the impressive revenue growth.

news.title: Morbi civic body withdraws slum demolition order after residents approach Gujarat HC
news.para: Morbi Municipal Corporation has halted demolition plans for a Panchasar Road slum after residents challenged the order in Gujarat High Court. Petitioners argued for alternative housing, citing decades of residence and government acknowledgment during the 1979 Machchhu floods. The civic body agreed to withdraw the order and consider their plea for relocation.

news.title: Patna civic body's property tax collection hits all-time high, crosses ₹116-crore target
news.para: Patna Municipal Corporation achieved a record Rs125 crore in property tax collection for fiscal 2025-26, exceeding its target by Rs9 crore. This success was driven by improved enforcement and a one-time settlement scheme offering a 100% waiver on interest and penalties, encouraging major defaulters to clear decades-old arrears.

news.title: Raymond Realty's pre-sales jump over two-fold to ₹1,519 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Raymond Realty reported a significant jump in sales bookings, reaching Rs 1,519 crore in the March quarter, more than double the previous year's Rs 636 crore. For the full fiscal year 2025-26, sales bookings rose 31% to Rs 3,023 crore.

news.title: Vijayawada civic body posts ₹267 crore tax collection in FY26
news.para: Vijayawada Municipal Corporation achieved a significant tax collection of Rs 267.10 crore for the 2025-26 financial year. This marks a Rs 15 crore improvement over the previous year's collection.  The civic body's efforts in tracing vacant land tax owners and taking lawful actions contributed to this revenue growth. The target for the upcoming financial year is set at 90% collection.

news.title: RPS Group to invest ₹110 crore in plotted township in Bahadurgarh
news.para: RPS Group is investing Rs 110 crore in RPS Azalea, a 19.79-acre plotting project in Bahadurgarh's Sector 29 under the DDJAY scheme. Construction begins immediately, with delivery expected in 2027. The project will also feature a 2-acre clubhouse and offers plots ranging from 125-180 sq yards, benefiting from excellent regional connectivity.

news.title: Belagavi: Property tax collection of BCC rises to ₹70.8 crore, but target remains elusive
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation collected Rs 70.8 crore in property tax for FY 2025-26, falling short of its Rs 89.4 crore target. Officials cited election duties and census work as reasons for the shortfall. The target for FY 2026-27 is projected to exceed Rs 92 crore, with ongoing census work posing further challenges.
